title: elymus barystachyus
intro: Elymus barystachyus, also known as barystachys or Chinese wild rye, is a type of perennial grass native to China and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to clear heat and relieve fever, making it a traditional remedy for conditions such as malaria and enteritis. In terms of health benefits, Elymus barystachyus is said to help alleviate symptoms associated with heat stroke, promote digestion, and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, although modern research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
